title: coprosma obconica subsp. obconica
intro: Coprosma obconica subsp. obconica, also known as the Kohuhu plant, is a native New Zealand shrub belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Coprosma obconica subsp. obconica include treating sore throats, coughs, and colds, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and menstrual cramps. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and salves.
